title: DATABASE Schema — Per-Workspace Portfolio Adoption Cutover to vector_artefacts
topic: Consolidated database design for the portfolio model adoption cutover from legacy mmff_vector.obj_* tables to vector_artefacts. Establishes per-workspace portfolio architecture (Tenant → Workspace → Model), single-table artefact_types substrate (strategy + work merged, parent_type_id chain), three-tier scope discriminator (global/tenant/workspace) on artefact_field_library, workspace whitelisting via artefact_workspace_fields join, master_record_portfolio (model fields only), drop of strategy_layers_adopted, re-adoption placeholder flow that preserves orphaned work items, adoption saga rewrite, frontend cutover, and gradual mmff_vector sanitisation register. Naming convention: domain prefix on every new table for procurement audit transparency.
summary: Per-workspace portfolio adoption rebuilt on vector_artefacts: single artefact_types substrate, scope-discriminated EAV, master_record_portfolio, gradual legacy drop.
content:
Contents
  1. 1. Overview
  2. 2. Architecture
  3. 3. Naming Rules
  4. 4. Schema Changes
  5. 5. EAV Field Substrate
  6. 6. master_record_portfolio
  7. 7. Adoption Saga Rewrite
  8. 8. Re-adoption Flow
  9. 9. Frontend Cutover
  10. 10. Sanitisation Register
  11. 11. Migration Order
  12. 12. Risks & Gaps
  13. 13. Story Decomposition

1. Overview

The portfolio model adoption flow currently writes to legacy mmff_vector.obj_* tables and reads model description prose live from mmff_library at render time. Both are wrong for the new substrate: vector_artefacts is the canonical tenant database, and the library DB must never be read at runtime by tenant-facing surfaces. This design rewires adoption to write to vector_artefacts, introduces per-workspace portfolio scoping, and consolidates strategy and work artefact types into a single artefact_types table with a parent_type_id chain. Provenance from the library catalogue is preserved as plain columns on artefact_types rather than via a separate strategy_layers_adopted mapping table, which is dropped.

Four guiding principles drive every decision below: (1) per-workspace scoping on every new artefact-domain row; (2) explicit scope discriminator instead of nullable trapdoors; (3) domain prefix on every new table name for procurement audit transparency (finance and defence); (4) gradual sanitisation of mmff_vector — never a big-bang demolition.

2. Architecture

Hierarchy:

Tenant (mmff_vector.subscriptions  →  becoming master_record_tenant)
  └─ Workspace A | Workspace B | Workspace * (mmff_vector.workspaces, retained for now)
        └─ Portfolio Model (vector_artefacts.master_record_portfolio, ONE row per workspace)
              ├─ Strategy artefact_types (scope='strategy', parent_type_id chain — depth 1..10)
              └─ Work artefact_types     (scope='work',     parent_type_id chain — depth 1..N)

Each workspace owns its own portfolio architecture. Two workspaces under the same tenant may run completely different models — different layer counts, different field schemas, different terminology — without interference. The model is hung below the workspace, not below the tenant.

Cross-database references (workspace_id in vector_artefacts pointing at mmff_vector.workspaces.id, model_id pointing at mmff_library.portfolio_templates.id) follow the existing pattern used by vector_artefacts.artefacts.workspace_id: no enforced FK, app-layer integrity only. This is the same trade-off already accepted across the substrate.

3. Naming Rules

Every new table in the artefact substrate carries the artefact_ prefix. Generic-sounding names are rejected on sight because they hide subsystem ownership and ambush future readers — a serious problem under procurement audit conditions in finance and defence. The single grep \bartefact_ must return every table in the substrate.

Generic name (rejected)Prefixed name (canonical)
field_libraryartefact_field_library
workspace_fieldsartefact_workspace_fields
strategy_layers_adopted(dropped — provenance lives on artefact_types as columns)

Master single-row-per-scope tables follow the master_record_* family: master_record_tenant (one row per tenant), master_record_portfolio (one row per workspace). Flow tables (flows, flow_states, flow_transitions) are a self-contained subsystem and follow their own naming pattern — they are not renamed.

4. Schema Changes

4.1 artefact_types — additive change

ColumnTypeNotes
workspace_iduuid NOT NULLNEW — per-workspace scope on every row
library_layer_iduuid NULLNEW — provenance, points at mmff_library.portfolio_template_layer_definitions.id
library_layer_tagtext NULLNEW — denormalised tag (e.g. SO, PO, BE, BC, FE) for fast filtering
scopetext NOT NULLexisting — values: strategy | work
sourcetext NOT NULLexisting — provenance source enum (library | tenant_authored | placeholder)
parent_type_iduuid NULLexisting — chain pointer; root layer has NULL
layer_depthinteger NOT NULLexisting — 1-based depth from root
prefixtext NOT NULLexisting — short tag for IDs (e.g. EP, US)
nametext NOT NULLexisting — display label
sort_orderinteger NOT NULLexisting — within-scope ordering
archived_attimestamptz NULLexisting — soft archive

New indexes:

CREATE INDEX idx_artefact_types_ws_scope_sort
  ON vector_artefacts.artefact_types (workspace_id, scope, sort_order)
  WHERE archived_at IS NULL;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_artefact_types_ws_scope_prefix
  ON vector_artefacts.artefact_types (workspace_id, scope, prefix)
  WHERE archived_at IS NULL;

4.2 field_library → artefact_field_library — rename + scope discriminator

ColumnTypeNotes
iduuid PKexisting
scopetext NOT NULLNEW — values: global | tenant | workspace
subscription_iduuid NULLbecomes nullable; NULL only when scope='global'
field_nametext NOT NULLexisting
data_typetext NOT NULLexisting — text|number|date|boolean|enum|reference
configjsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'existing — type-specific config (enum options, ref target, validation)
archived_attimestamptz NULLexisting

Constraints:

ALTER TABLE vector_artefacts.artefact_field_library
  ADD CONSTRAINT chk_afl_scope_values
    CHECK (scope IN ('global','tenant','workspace')),
  ADD CONSTRAINT chk_afl_global_no_subscription
    CHECK ( (scope = 'global'    AND subscription_id IS NULL)
         OR (scope IN ('tenant','workspace') AND subscription_id IS NOT NULL) );

Why explicit scope rather than NULL=global: NULL semantics rot under load. WHERE subscription_id IS NULL for "global" tangles every query with NULL-handling branches and is invisible in audit reports. WHERE scope = 'global' reads cleanly, indexes cleanly, and surfaces in audit logs as a discrete value finance and defence reviewers can challenge directly.

4.3 strategy_layers_adopted — DROP

Provenance moves to artefact_types.library_layer_id + library_layer_tag. The mapping table added zero behavioural value once artefact_types already carried workspace_id, scope, and parent_type_id. The drop is gradual: the table stays in place until the last reader (adopt.go) is rewritten, then a follow-up migration removes it.

4.4 artefact_workspace_fields — NEW (workspace whitelist)

Used only when artefact_field_library.scope = 'workspace'. Expresses "field is valid in workspace A and C but not B" — a strong audit primitive for procurement transparency. Absence of a row equals exclusion (deny by default).

ColumnTypeNotes
workspace_iduuid NOT NULLcross-DB reference to mmff_vector.workspaces.id
field_library_iduuid NOT NULLFK artefact_field_library(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
created_attimestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()—
created_byuuid NULLuser who whitelisted the field
PRIMARY KEY (workspace_id, field_library_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_awf_field ON vector_artefacts.artefact_workspace_fields (field_library_id);

5. EAV Field Substrate

The four-table substrate already exists for artefact extension — only artefact_field_library changes shape. The flow is unchanged in spirit:

TableRole
artefact_typesDefines the kind of artefact (Epic, Story, Defect, Strategic Goal). Owns the parent chain.
artefact_type_fieldsPer-type schema row — which fields apply to this kind, in what order, with what defaults.
artefact_field_libraryField definitions — name, data type, config. Scoped global/tenant/workspace.
artefact_field_valuesThe values themselves, keyed (artefact_id, field_id).
artefact_workspace_fieldsWhitelist for scope='workspace' entries — which workspaces may use this field.

Resolution algorithm at write time, given (workspace_id, artefact_type_id, field_name):

  1. Find the artefact_field_library row for field_name.
  2. If scope = 'global' → admit.
  3. If scope = 'tenant' → require subscription_id matches the workspace's tenant.
  4. If scope = 'workspace' → require a row in artefact_workspace_fields (workspace_id, field_library_id).
  5. Otherwise reject with a 4xx, surface in audit log.

6. master_record_portfolio

One row per workspace. Holds the portfolio model persistent record only — no workspace identity duplication. Workspace name, description, and owner remain in mmff_vector.workspaces until that table is itself sanitised in a later, deliberate cutover.

ColumnTypeNotes
workspace_iduuid PRIMARY KEYcross-DB reference to mmff_vector.workspaces.id
model_iduuid NULLcross-DB reference to mmff_library.portfolio_templates.id
model_nametext NOT NULLcopied at adoption — survives library deletion
model_descriptiontext NULLprose copied at adoption — replaces the live read from mmff_library
adopted_attimestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()—
adopted_by_user_iduuid NULL—
created_attimestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()—
updated_attimestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()maintained by trigger
archived_attimestamptz NULLsoft archive

Trigger trg_master_record_portfolio_touch_updated_at mirrors the pattern from master_record_tenant (function fn_master_record_portfolio_touch_updated_at sets NEW.updated_at = now() on UPDATE).

7. Adoption Saga Rewrite

The current 7-step saga in backend/internal/portfolio/adopt.go writes to legacy obj_* tables in mmff_vector. Each step is rewired to vector_artefacts:

StepOld target (mmff_vector)New target (vector_artefacts)
1. validate——
2. layersobj_strategy_types_layersartefact_types (scope=strategy, parent chain by parent_type_id)
3. workflowssubscription_workflowsflows
4. transitionssubscription_workflow_transitionsflow_transitions
5. artifactssubscription_artifactsartefact_types (scope=work)
6. terminologysubscription_terminology(deferred — open question 12.2)
7. finalize—INSERT/UPDATE master_record_portfolio

Each step copies provenance from the library row into the new artefact_types row: library_layer_id from portfolio_template_layer_definitions.id, library_layer_tag from the matching tag column, source = 'library'. The model description prose is copied into master_record_portfolio.model_description at step 7 — never read live afterwards.

The saga is wrapped in a single transaction per step in vector_artefacts. SSE progress events stream after each step commits. Idempotency keys remain at the saga level so a re-fired adoption is a no-op rather than a duplicate insert.

8. Re-adoption Flow

Re-adoption is the case where a workspace already has a model and switches to a different one. The hard requirement: existing work artefacts must survive with their parent relationships intact, even though the strategy chain changes underneath them.

  1. Identify all current artefact_types rows where scope='strategy' for the workspace.
  2. Insert a single placeholder strategy artefact at the bottom layer of the OLD chain (source='placeholder', name='Pending re-adoption', library_layer_id=NULL).
  3. UPDATE every existing work artefacts row whose parent_artefact_id pointed at the now-deprecated bottom strategy artefact → repoint at the placeholder.
  4. Delete old strategy artefacts rows (now zero references — the orphans were just re-parented).
  5. Delete old strategy artefact_types rows. artefact_type_fields CASCADEs.
  6. Insert the new strategy chain via the standard adoption saga (steps 2–4).
  7. UPDATE master_record_portfolio with the new model_id / model_name / model_description and stamp adopted_at = now().
  8. Surface the placeholder in the UI as a visible "needs re-classification" bin so the workspace owner can manually move work items into the new model.

The placeholder pattern keeps parent_artefact_id NOT NULL on work items — the schema invariant never breaks during re-adoption.

9. Frontend Cutover

SurfaceOld readNew read
BundleView model descriptionGET /api/library/portfolio_templates/:id (mmff_library)GET /api/portfolio/master_record (vector_artefacts.master_record_portfolio for current workspace)
Adoption streamGET /api/portfolio/adopt-stream/:workspaceId (writes obj_*)same endpoint, rewritten saga writes vector_artefacts
Adoption stateGET /api/portfolio/adoption-state/:workspaceId (reads obj_strategy_types_layers)same endpoint, rewritten to read artefact_types + master_record_portfolio
Layer listGET /api/subscription/layers (subscription-scoped)GET /api/workspace/:id/portfolio/layers (workspace-scoped)
Field schemadirect DB read of field_libraryGET /api/workspace/:id/fields with scope-resolution applied server-side

No frontend component reads mmff_library directly after this cutover. The library DB becomes a build-time content source consumed by the adoption saga only — never a runtime dependency for tenant-facing pages.

10. Sanitisation Register (mmff_vector)

Tracked in docs/c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md. Each row stays read-only or write-frozen until the trigger condition fires, then a follow-up migration drops it. Never deleted in the same PR that introduces the replacement.

Legacy table (mmff_vector)Trigger to dropStatus
obj_strategy_types_layersLast reader is rewritten in adopt.goopen
subscription_workflowsAdoption saga step 3 cuts over to flowsopen
subscription_workflow_transitionsAdoption saga step 4 cuts over to flow_transitionsopen
subscription_artifactsAdoption saga step 5 cuts over to artefact_types (work)open
subscription_terminologyTerminology surface migrated (open question 12.2)deferred
strategy_layers_adoptedProvenance now lives on artefact_types columnsopen
obj_* work-item residuev2 work-items live on vector_artefacts.artefacts for ≥7 daysopen
workspaces (mmff_vector)Workspace identity moved to vector_artefacts.master_record_workspace in a future cutovernot started

11. Migration Order

One migration per landing, in this order. Each runs through backend/cmd/migrate against vector_artefacts.

  1. NNN_artefact_field_library_rename.sql — RENAME field_library → artefact_field_library; ALTER for scope column with backfill (UPDATE … SET scope = CASE WHEN subscription_id IS NULL THEN 'global' ELSE 'tenant' END); add CHECK constraints; ALTER subscription_id NULL.
  2. NNN_artefact_workspace_fields.sql — CREATE artefact_workspace_fields with PK and index.
  3. NNN_artefact_types_workspace_scope.sql — ADD workspace_id, library_layer_id, library_layer_tag columns to artefact_types; backfill workspace_id from prior tenant→default-workspace mapping; new indexes.
  4. NNN_master_record_portfolio.sql — CREATE master_record_portfolio + trigger function + trigger.
  5. NNN_adoption_saga_rewrite.sql — backfill master_record_portfolio rows for currently-adopted workspaces (read existing obj_strategy_types_layers + subscription_artifacts + library prose, INSERT one row per workspace).
  6. NNN_drop_strategy_layers_adopted.sql — DROP strategy_layers_adopted after the adopt.go rewrite has been deployed for ≥1 day.
  7. Sanitisation drops (one migration per legacy table, only after each replacement is verified live for ≥7 days).

12. Risks & Gaps

12.1 Cross-DB integrity

workspace_id in vector_artefacts tables has no enforced FK to mmff_vector.workspaces. A workspace deletion in mmff_vector would orphan rows in vector_artefacts. Mitigation: workspace deletion is already a soft-archive operation app-side; add a deletion-policy hook that scans vector_artefacts for orphans on archive and rejects if any remain. Deferred to a follow-up story.

12.2 Terminology table

Adoption saga step 6 (terminology) has no obvious target in vector_artefacts. Open design: either add artefact_terminology (workspace-scoped key/value table) or fold terminology into artefact_types.name + a per-type display-overrides JSONB. Deferred to a brainstorm before storification.

12.3 Re-adoption placeholder UX

The placeholder strategy artefact will appear in the UI as a re-classification bucket. UX flow for moving items out of the placeholder is not designed yet — current scope only guarantees data survival, not a smooth user experience around re-classification. Deferred to a frontend story under PLA-0026.

12.4 Library DB future role

After this cutover, mmff_library is consumed only by the adoption saga at adoption time. Whether the library remains co-located with the backend or migrates to a separate read-only catalogue service is an open question — out of scope for this design.

12.5 Backfill correctness

The migration step 5 backfill (one master_record_portfolio row per currently-adopted workspace) needs a parity test: count of adopted workspaces in legacy === count of master_record_portfolio rows post-migration. Story owner adds the parity assertion as part of the migration story.

12.6 Field-library scope backfill assumption

The migration assumes existing field_library rows with subscription_id IS NULL are global. If any rows were created with a NULL subscription_id for a different reason (e.g. a tenant-scoped field with a bug), they will be silently miscategorised as global. Mitigation: pre-migration audit query asserts there are no "orphan" NULL rows that map to a known tenant via another path.

13. Story Decomposition

Per the project's stories rule (hard): every observable layer carries its own card before /stories is invoked. PLA number to be allocated at storification time. Ordering across layers is loose — migrations land first within their stack, then the backend service that depends on each table, then the handler, then the frontend that calls the handler, with tests landing alongside their target.

Risk legend: S1 = data-loss risk (migrations, backfills, saga rewrites), S2 = behavioural regression risk (handler/UI cutovers), S3 = mechanical (UI URL swaps, additive table creates).

13.1 Migration layer (database)

#StoryRiskNotes
M1Pre-migration audit: assert zero field_library rows with subscription_id IS NULL that map to a known tenant via any other pathS1Gate for M2 — fix any miscategorised rows manually before migrating
M2Rename field_library → artefact_field_library; add scope column with backfill (NULL → 'global', else 'tenant'); make subscription_id nullable; add CHECK constraintsS1Includes DOWN migration; touches every reader call-site rename
M3Create artefact_workspace_fields (PK, FK to artefact_field_library, index)S3Additive only
M4Extend artefact_types with workspace_id NOT NULL, library_layer_id NULL, library_layer_tag NULL; backfill workspace_id from prior tenant→default-workspace mapping; add (workspace_id, scope, sort_order) index + (workspace_id, scope, prefix) unique indexS1Backfill must be deterministic and parity-tested before NOT NULL is enforced
M5Create master_record_portfolio table + fn_master_record_portfolio_touch_updated_at + triggerS3Additive — mirrors master_record_tenant pattern
M6Backfill master_record_portfolio for currently-adopted workspaces (read legacy obj_strategy_types_layers + subscription_artifacts + library prose, INSERT one row per workspace)S1Includes parity assertion (count match) — see T1
M7Drop strategy_layers_adopted (gated on B5 deployed ≥1 day)S2Follow-up PR — never land in same PR as B5

13.2 Backend layer (Go)

#StoryRiskNotes
B1portfolio.MasterRecordService — CRUD on master_record_portfolio (sole writer boundary, mirrors internal/tenant.Service)S2Depends on M5
B2Field scope resolver — server-side (workspace_id, field_id) → admit/deny against artefact_field_library.scope + artefact_workspace_fieldsS2Depends on M2 + M3
B3Adoption saga step 2 (layers) rewrite: write artefact_types scope=strategy with library_layer_id + library_layer_tag provenanceS1Depends on M4; carries idempotency key
B4Adoption saga step 3+4 (workflows + transitions) rewrite: write flows + flow_transitionsS1Within same saga transaction
B5Adoption saga step 5 (artifacts) rewrite: write artefact_types scope=work with parent chainS1Depends on M4
B6Adoption saga step 7 (finalize): INSERT/UPDATE master_record_portfolio with copied model_id, model_name, model_description proseS1Depends on B1; the prose copy replaces the live library read
B7Adoption saga: SSE progress events refitted for new step targets; idempotency keys preservedS2Cross-cuts B3–B6
B8Re-adoption flow: insert placeholder strategy artefact at OLD bottom layer, repoint orphaned work artefacts.parent_artefact_id, delete old strategy rows, run new adoptionS1The hard requirement: parent_artefact_id NOT NULL never breaks during re-adoption
B9Handler GET /api/portfolio/master_record (workspace-scoped read of master_record_portfolio)S3Depends on B1
B10Handler GET /api/workspace/:id/portfolio/layers — replaces /api/subscription/layers; reads artefact_types scope=strategy for the workspaceS2Old endpoint stays live until F3 lands
B11Handler GET /api/workspace/:id/fields — invokes B2 scope resolver server-sideS2Depends on B2
B12Handler GET /api/portfolio/adoption-state/:workspaceId — rewrite to read artefact_types + master_record_portfolio instead of obj_strategy_types_layersS2Currently 404s post-reset (per memory) — fix in this story
B13Workspace deletion hook: scan vector_artefacts for orphans on archive, reject if any remain (deferred per 12.1)S2Deferred follow-up — file but do not block on it

13.3 Frontend layer (Next.js / React)

#StoryRiskNotes
F1BundleView: switch model description read from /api/library/portfolio_templates/:id → /api/portfolio/master_recordS2Depends on B9; removes the runtime mmff_library dependency
F2Adoption stream: verify SSE event handling renders correctly against rewritten saga; no code change expected, but assertion test requiredS3Depends on B7
F3Layer list: swap /api/subscription/layers → /api/workspace/:id/portfolio/layers across all callers (and any default-fallback paths)S2Depends on B10; grep call-sites and update together
F4Field schema consumer: switch to /api/workspace/:id/fields; remove direct DB reads of field_library from any frontend codeS2Depends on B11
F5Re-adoption UX: surface the placeholder strategy artefact as a visible "Pending re-classification" bucket with a move-out flow (deferred per 12.3)S2Deferred follow-up — file under PLA-0026 but do not block landing

13.4 Test layer

#StoryRiskNotes
T1Migration parity test: count of legacy adopted workspaces === count of master_record_portfolio rows post-M6S1Lands inside M6 — gate for promotion
T2Pre-migration audit assertion: zero NULL subscription_id orphansS1Lands inside M1
T3Integration test: adoption saga end-to-end writes only to vector_artefacts (assert zero writes to obj_*)S1Cross-cuts B3–B7
T4Integration test: re-adoption flow — orphan work items repointed, placeholder visible, no parent_artefact_id NULL, no FK violationS1Depends on B8
T5Integration test: scope resolver admit/deny matrix — (global, tenant-match, tenant-mismatch, workspace-whitelisted, workspace-not-whitelisted) × (read, write)S2Depends on B2
T6Canary test: cross-DB workspace_id integrity — every vector_artefacts row's workspace_id exists in mmff_vector.workspacesS2Schedules nightly
T7Lint rule lint:portfolio-library-read — fails on any frontend file importing /api/library/ URLs in tenant code pathsS2Lands with F1

13.5 Sanitisation follow-ups (legacy mmff_vector)

One PR per legacy table, only after each replacement is verified live for ≥7 days. Tracked in docs/c_c_v2_workitems_cutover_followups.md. Filed as cards but not blocking the main cutover.

#DropTrigger
S1obj_strategy_types_layersB3 deployed ≥7 days, zero reads observed
S2subscription_workflows + subscription_workflow_transitionsB4 deployed ≥7 days
S3subscription_artifactsB5 deployed ≥7 days
S4strategy_layers_adoptedB5 deployed ≥1 day (M7)
S5subscription_terminologyDeferred until terminology design lands (12.2)

13.6 Out-of-scope / brainstorm-first

  • Terminology table design (12.2) — needs its own brainstorm before storification; either artefact_terminology table or artefact_types JSONB overrides
  • Library DB future role (12.4) — separate decision, out of this PLA
  • Workspace identity migration — moving mmff_vector.workspaces → vector_artefacts.master_record_workspace is a separate cutover, gated on this one landing first

13.7 Tally
